evals.name: adapts-to-project-type
evals.prompt: Document the architecture of this project. Scan the codebase and generate architecture docs.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the project as a web API (Express + TypeScript). Produces a tech stack table sourced from package.json. Includes a mermaid system overview diagram. Documents the actual directory structure (src/routes/, src/models/). Does NOT invent layers that don't exist.

assertions.description: Identifies the project as a web API (Express-based or similar classification)
Produces a tech stack table with entries sourced from the actual package.json (Express, TypeScript, Prisma, Jest)
Includes at least one mermaid diagram (system overview flowchart showing component relationships)
Documents the actual directory structure including src/routes/ and src/models/ — not a generic template
Does NOT document layers that don't exist in the codebase (e.g., frontend, messaging, infrastructure) — only what's actually present

evals.name: single-file-for-simple-project
evals.prompt: Analyze this project's architecture and create documentation for it.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the project as a CLI tool. Produces a single ARCHITECTURE.md (not a multi-file architecture/ directory) because the project is simple with ≤3 layers. Does NOT invent complex layer structures for a simple CLI tool.

assertions.description: Identifies the project as a CLI tool (not a web API, SPA, or other type)
Produces a single ARCHITECTURE.md file rather than a multi-file architecture/ directory — the project is too simple for multiple files
Does NOT document complex layer structures (API, data, frontend, infra) — lists only what actually exists in this simple project

evals.name: notes-code-smells-objectively
evals.prompt: Document the architecture of this codebase. I want to understand how it's structured.
evals.expected_output: Scans the code and notes that billing imports directly from auth internals (cross-feature coupling). Notes this objectively as an observation, not as a judgment. Uses neutral language like 'imports directly from' rather than 'terrible design'.

assertions.description: Detects that billing imports from auth (cross-feature coupling between features/billing and features/auth)
Notes the coupling objectively using neutral/descriptive language — does NOT use judgmental words like 'terrible', 'bad', 'wrong', or 'awful'
Does NOT prescribe specific refactoring steps or recommend architectural changes — that is propose-architecture's job. May note the observation but does not turn it into an action plan.

evals.name: asks-for-corrections
evals.prompt: Generate architecture documentation for this project from the source code.
evals.expected_output: After generating the documentation, presents a summary of findings and asks the user for corrections or feedback before finalizing. Does NOT just silently write files without asking.

assertions.description: Presents a summary of findings (project type, layers found, or document structure chosen) before or alongside the generated docs
Explicitly asks the user for corrections, feedback, or confirmation — does not just silently finalize without user input
